solution.p: We will factor x^{2}-22x+121 where a=1, b=-22, and c=121.

Perfect Square Formula: The formula for perfect square trinomials is (a \pm b)^2=a^2 \pm 2ab + b^2. Note that the polynomial x^{2}-22x+121 matches the formula's right side, and we have: \begin{aligned}[t]x^{2}-22x+121\amp =\left(x\right)^2-2\left(x\right)\left(11\right)+\left(11\right)^2\newline \amp =\left(x-11\right)\mathopen{}\left(x-11\right)\newline \amp =\left(x-11\right)^{2}\end{aligned}
Mental Method: When we factor a polynomial in the form of x^2+bx+c, x^2+bxy+cy^2, or x^2y^2+bxy+c we can use mental math to factor. We need to find two numbers whose product is c, and whose sum is b.

For x^{2}-22x+121, since the product of -11 and -11 is 121, and the sum of -11 and -11 is -22, the factorization is \begin{aligned}[t]x^{2}-22x+121\amp =\left(x-11\right)\mathopen{}\left(x-11\right)\newline \amp =\left(x-11\right)^{2}\end{aligned}
AC Method: In this method, we first calculate the product of a and c in ax^2+bx+c. For this problem, we have (1) \cdot (121) = 121.

The number 121 can be factored into the product of two numbers in the following ways:  egin{array}{lll}1(121)\amp -1(-121)\amp 11(11)\amp -11(-11)
ewline\end{array} Looking at the corresponding sums, we want a sum of -22, and this sum happens with (-11)+(-11)=-22 So we write  egin{aligned}[t]x^{2}-22x+121\amp =x^{2}-11x-11x+121
ewline \amp =x\mathopen{}\left(x-11ight)-11\mathopen{}\left(x-11ight)
ewline \amp =\left(x-11ight)\mathopen{}\left(x-11ight)
ewline \amp =\left(x-11ight)^{2}\end{aligned}Generic Rectangles Method: First, we set up generic rectangles by putting the first term and third term in x^{2}-22x+121 into the top left and bottom right rectangles:
The first term's coefficient, 1, can be factored into the product of two numbers (where the first factor is positive) in the following ways: \begin{array}{llll}1(1)\amp \end{array}
The third term, 121, can be factored into the product of two numbers in the following ways: \begin{array}{llll}1(121)\amp -1(-121)\amp 11(11)\amp -11(-11)\newline\end{array}

We put each pair into the corresponding places next to those generic rectangles, and try to match the area of those rectangles with x^{2}-22x+121 by guess-and-check. The following generic rectangles show the solution:

Dimensions of those generic rectangles in the diagram give us the solution: \begin{aligned}[t]x^{2}-22x+121\amp =\left(x-11\right)\mathopen{}\left(x-11\right)\newline \amp =\left(x-11\right)^{2}\end{aligned}
